name: Search existing consignee
caption: Traders
heading: Search for an existing consignee
layout: govuk-grid-row > govuk-grid-column-full (search fieldset inputs laid out three-up in govuk-grid-column-one-third columns)

structure.text: Traders search results table (govuk-visually-hidden caption for the results)
Results table, columns: Name | Address | Country | View | Select. Each data row's View and Select cells contain a button styled as a link (link-button).
structure.govukComponent: Table (govuk-table) — but action cells use custom link-button, not standard

govukComponents.usage: Page H1 'Search for an existing consignee'
Groups the three search fields (Name, Address, Country) under legend 'Search'
Name and Address free-text search fields
Country filter (254 <option> elements: 1 'Select country' placeholder + 253 selectable entries)

Plain govuk-label (no modifier) for the three search fields Name, Address, Country. Separately, govuk-label--s is used on the ~75 <h3> card heads in the mobile card layout — NOT on these field labels.
govukComponents.evidence: trace eddc6f6ec852d692c454d319ade1607727a84a4f action 55 (DOM eval: label[for=name|address|country].className === 'govuk-label'; 75 elements with class govuk-label--s, all H3)

govukComponents.usage: Desktop results table: Name | Address | Country | View | Select. Note the View/Select action cells contain custom link-button controls, not govuk-button

'Create a new consignee' link (id add-economic-operator) below the results, navigates to /traders/consignee/new. This is the ONLY control on this page the QA tests interact with — every CHED-D journey clicks it immediately to bypass the search.
govukComponents.evidence: trace eddc6f6ec852d692c454d319ade1607727a84a4f action 55; corroborated trace b3742f19b6eea5d9285f5c8739571523a0a13ec1 action 59; inferred corroboration page-objects/notification/SearchExistingConsigneePage.ts:6-8 (getByRole('link', { name: 'Create a new consignee' })), driven at workflows/notification/ched-d-workflows.ts:270 and tests/accessibility/ched-d-accessibility-tests.spec.ts:117

nonStandardPatterns.whatItDoes: The per-row 'View' and 'Select' controls are <button> elements styled to look like links via a custom 'link-button' class. Each sits in its own <form> cell so clicking submits/navigates for that specific consignee.
nonStandardPatterns.concern: Custom class outside the Design System. Buttons visually disguised as links is a known GOV.UK anti-pattern; either a real link or a standard govuk-button should be used. Semantically 'Select' is the primary action per row and 'View' opens the party detail.
nonStandardPatterns.govukAlternative: govuk-button (or govuk-button--secondary) for the actions, or a plain link where navigation-only. In the rebuild the selection could be a single govuk-button per row.
nonStandardPatterns.evidence: trace eddc6f6ec852d692c454d319ade1607727a84a4f action 55 (structure eval: BUTTON.link-button :: View / Select)

nonStandardPatterns.pattern: pagination / pagination-list / pagination-item / pagination-item-next / pagination-link / pagination-link-icon / pagination-link-label / pagination-link-title
nonStandardPatterns.whatItDoes: Bespoke pagination widget rendering 'Next page : 2 of 4000' with an icon, linking to ?page=2. Not the govuk-pagination component.
nonStandardPatterns.concern: Entirely custom pagination markup predating (or ignoring) the GOV.UK Pagination component. '2 of 4000' implies the address book returns huge unfiltered result sets that must be paged — a data/UX concern for the rebuild (the search returns thousands of near-duplicate seed rows here).
nonStandardPatterns.govukAlternative: govuk-pagination (Pagination component) with previous/next and numbered pages.
nonStandardPatterns.evidence: trace eddc6f6ec852d692c454d319ade1607727a84a4f action 55 (class inventory + a11y navigation 'Next page : 2 of 4000')

nonStandardPatterns.whatItDoes: Dual rendering of the results: a desktop govuk-table (traders-search-results-form-desktop) and a separate mobile card layout (traders-search-results-form-mobile / transporter-table-mobile) that repeats each row as H3 (govuk-label--s) label + paragraph value stacks for Name/Address/Country plus View/Select buttons.
nonStandardPatterns.concern: Bespoke responsive-table pattern with duplicated DOM for mobile vs desktop, and a leftover 'transporter-table-mobile' class name (copy-pasted from the transporter page). Custom, not a Design System pattern. Doubles the markup and the maintenance surface.
nonStandardPatterns.govukAlternative: A single govuk-table (which is responsive), or the GOV.UK responsive-table pattern; avoid duplicating rows for mobile.
nonStandardPatterns.evidence: trace eddc6f6ec852d692c454d319ade1607727a84a4f action 55 (class inventory + structure eval showing repeated H3/P card stacks)

nonStandardPatterns.pattern: search-panel / name-address-and-country / form-group / economic-operator-name / economic-operator-address / link-button
nonStandardPatterns.whatItDoes: Custom wrapper classes for the search form panel and its field grouping (name-address-and-country lays the three inputs out three-up). economic-operator-name/address are id/class hooks on the results.
nonStandardPatterns.concern: IPAFFS-specific layout/styling hooks rather than standard govuk-form-group / grid usage. 'form-group' (unprefixed) duplicates govuk-form-group. Cosmetic but signals custom CSS the rebuild need not carry.
nonStandardPatterns.govukAlternative: govuk-grid-row + govuk-grid-column-* and govuk-form-group only; no bespoke panel classes needed.

fields.name: name
fields.label: Name
fields.conditionalOn: always shown (search filter). Rendered for consignee because economicOperatorSettings.consignee.inputs = [name, address, country] (legacy service/src/utils/economic_operator.js:54-58).
fields.requiredEvidence: legacy — optional search filter: Joi schema declares name as Joi.string().max(255).allow('') with no .required() (service/src/validation/routes/handlers/importer/traders/search.js:20-22). Confirms the trace-observed non-required state is deliberate.
fields.evidence: trace eddc6f6ec852d692c454d319ade1607727a84a4f action 55 (INPUT|text|name|name|false). NOT exercised by any CHED-D test: the page object SearchExistingConsigneePage.ts:6-8 exposes no locator for this input, and every workflow (ched-d-workflows.ts:270) / accessibility test (ched-d-accessibility-tests.spec.ts:117) clicks 'Create a new consignee' immediately. Trace-confirmed rendered reality, test-uncorroborated. Legacy corroboration: template partial service/src/views/importer/traders/search.html:26-36 (govuk/textInput id=name, gated on inputs 'name'); 255-char cap legacy search.js:20-22.

fields.name: address
fields.label: Address
fields.conditionalOn: always shown (search filter). Rendered for consignee per economicOperatorSettings.consignee.inputs (legacy economic_operator.js:54-58).
fields.requiredEvidence: legacy — optional search filter: Joi schema declares address as Joi.string().max(255).allow('') with no .required() (search.js:23-25).
fields.evidence: trace eddc6f6ec852d692c454d319ade1607727a84a4f action 55 (INPUT|text|address|address|false). NOT exercised by any CHED-D test (see 'name' field note): the 'select an existing consignee' branch is trace-confirmed only, test-uncorroborated. Legacy corroboration: template partial service/src/views/importer/traders/search.html:37-47 (govuk/textInput id=address); 255-char cap legacy search.js:23-25.

fields.name: country
fields.label: Country
fields.conditionalOn: always shown (search filter), but the whole country dropdown is additionally gated on showCountryDropDown = !isReimport(notification) AND-ed with inputs containing 'country' (legacy template service/src/views/importer/traders/search.html:73-83; handler service/src/routes/handlers/importer/traders/search.js:187). i.e. on a re-import journey the country filter is suppressed. For a standard CHED-D consignee search it is shown.
fields.requiredEvidence: legacy — optional search filter with a validity constraint: Joi schema declares country as Joi.string().valid(...countries).allow('') with no .required() (search.js:35-37). Empty is allowed; a non-empty value must match a known country/region reference-data code (the valid set is built at runtime from countriesService.getCountries + getUkRegions, search.js:155-161).
fields.evidence: trace eddc6f6ec852d692c454d319ade1607727a84a4f action 55 (SELECT|select-one|country|country|false; full option list in a11y snapshot; DOM eval confirms options.length === 254). First option 'Select country' is the empty placeholder (legacy template hardcodes <option value="">Select country</option>, search.html:78, then partial data/countriesSelectHighLow renders the reference-data list, search.html:79). List is country reference data (includes the four UK home nations England/Northern Ireland/Scotland/Wales as separate options) — the new app should source this from a reference-data service, not hardcode it. Inferred corroboration types/country.ts:238-241 (England GB-ENG, NorthernIreland GB-NIR, Scotland GB-SCT, Wales GB-WLS as distinct entries), confirming the home-nation split is real reference data, not a rendering artefact. Not exercised by any CHED-D test.

fields.label: View (per result row)
fields.name: view (link-button)
fields.conditionalOn: shown per consignee returned by the search
fields.evidence: trace eddc6f6ec852d692c454d319ade1607727a84a4f action 55 (BUTTON.link-button :: View, one per row). NOT exercised by any CHED-D test — no page-object locator; the results table is never interacted with. Trace-confirmed, test-uncorroborated.

fields.label: Select (per result row)
fields.name: select (link-button)
fields.conditionalOn: shown per consignee returned by the search; selecting attaches that party as the consignee
fields.evidence: trace eddc6f6ec852d692c454d319ade1607727a84a4f action 55 (BUTTON.link-button :: Select, one per row). NOT exercised by any CHED-D test — the 'select an existing consignee' path is never taken; every journey creates a new consignee instead. Trace-confirmed, test-uncorroborated.

validationMessages.message: Name must be 255 characters or fewer
validationMessages.evidence: Joi schema service/src/validation/routes/handlers/importer/traders/search.js:20-22 (Joi.string().max(255), STRING_MAX -> maxLengthMessage(labels.NAME, 255)); template service/src/validation/messages/en.js:3-5 (`${label} must be ${maxLength} characters or fewer`); label service/src/validation/labels/en.js:67 (NAME: 'Name'). Frontend Joi validation on the GET search form; not in the Java microservice ValidationMessages.properties (that governs notification submission, not this search page). NEVER rendered in any trace (both traces had 0 errors).

validationMessages.message: Address must be 255 characters or fewer
validationMessages.evidence: Joi schema search.js:23-25 (Joi.string().max(255), STRING_MAX -> maxLengthMessage(labels.ADDRESS, 255)); template messages/en.js:3-5; label labels/en.js:3 (ADDRESS: 'Address'). Never rendered in any trace.

validationMessages.trigger: country search filter submitted with a value that is not a known country/region reference-data code (empty is allowed)
validationMessages.evidence: Joi schema search.js:35-37 (Joi.string().valid(...countries).allow(''), ANY_VALID -> invalidMessage(labels.COUNTRY)); template messages/en.js:19-21 (`${field} is not valid`); label labels/en.js:34 (COUNTRY: 'Country'). Only reachable by tampering with the request since the UI is a fixed dropdown. Never rendered in any trace.

openQuestions: The QA automation NEVER exercises the search on this page. The page object page-objects/notification/SearchExistingConsigneePage.ts exposes a single locator — linkCreateNewConsignee (lines 6-8) — and every CHED-D journey clicks 'Create a new consignee' immediately (workflows/notification/ched-d-workflows.ts:270; tests/accessibility/ched-d-accessibility-tests.spec.ts:117). No CHED-D spec (tests/notification/ched-d/*.spec.ts) references the consignee search, its fields, or the results table. So the entire 'search then Select an existing party' branch (name/address/country filters + View/Select actions) is trace-confirmed rendered reality but has ZERO test corroboration. Whether searching and selecting an existing consignee is a required capability in the rebuild, or a legacy path that can be dropped in favour of always-create, is a gap for a human.

No error/validation state was captured for this page in either trace (both had 0 errors) and no test asserts any validation message here — but the legacy source now ANSWERS the 'does search require any input?' gap: the Joi validator (service/src/validation/routes/handlers/importer/traders/search.js) declares all three filters as .allow('') with NO .required(), so Search with every filter blank is valid (returns the full paged address book). The only validation is a 255-char cap on name and address and a valid-country-code check on country (see validationMessages). This is a GET-form search; the frontend Joi rules apply, not the Java notification-submission ValidationMessages.properties. Whether the rebuild keeps an unfiltered 'search everything' Search remains a UX gap given the 4000-page result sets.

The results table renders thousands of near-identical seed rows ('2 of 4000' pages). This is test seed data; real pagination behaviour and how results are scoped/filtered (e.g. by the logged-in user's address book) is not evidenced here.
The 'View' action's destination page (party detail/read-only view) was not exercised in these traces or by any test — it is a separate page (gap).
Hidden fields present on submit: crumb (CSRF), etag (optimistic concurrency), returnUrl, fromFooterHeader — platform plumbing, not user requirements, but noted for the rebuild.

Country list contains 254 options (a 'Select country' placeholder plus 253 selectable entries) including the four UK home nations as distinct entries; exact list is reference data (corroborated types/country.ts:238-241) and should not be hardcoded.
